info.title: REST API for Common Features in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications - Users
info.description:
Last Updated January 2026

You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

Whether you're experienced with or new to REST, use this guide to find what you need, including:

  • A quick start how-to that walks you through a simple request example.
  • Use cases that provide real-world solutions with code examples.
  • Detailed descriptions for each REST resource.

Some REST resources have more than one version. In the Tasks section, when you expand a resource that has more than one version, you'll notice that the main folder contains subfolders, such as 11.13.18.05 and v1 (latest), corresponding to the available resource versions. Resources that don't have a newer version continue to appear without any change in their folder structure. You can find additional details on how to work with the different resource versions in the Quick Start and other topics in the Get Started section.
info.x-summary: Last Updated January 2026 You can use Oracle REST APIs to view and manage data stored in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the query expression to retrieve a subset of user resource instances. Format: ?filter=<scim-filter>  Example: filter=firstName pr and userType eq Employee.

parameters.name: startIndex
parameters.description: This parameter specifies use of 1-based index for the first result included in the response. Specify this query parameter to use pagination.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the number of resource instances to be returned in the current range.

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the attributes based on which the resource collection is sorted.Format:  ?sortBy=<scim-attribute> where the attribute name must be of the format <attribute> for simple attributes and <attribute.subattribute> for complex attributes. Example: sortBy=active

parameters.name: sortOrder
parameters.description: This parameter specifies the attributes based on which the resource collection is ordered. Example: sortOrder=ascending

parameters.name: attributes
parameters.description: This parameter specifies a comma-separated list of attribute values to be included in the response.

204.description: No content. This task does not return elements in the response body.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the user.

requestBody.description: The following table describes the body parameters in the request for this task.

200.description: The following table describes the response for this task.

itemsPerPage.title: Count
itemsPerPage.description: The number of resource instances returned in the current range.

startIndex.title: start index
startIndex.description: This parameter defines the starting position in the resource collection. For example, if startIndex is 11 and itemsPerPage is 10, the response body contains rows from 11 to 20.

Resources.title: Resources
Resources.description: The user resources in the collection.

id.title: Id
id.description: A unique identifier for the user resource in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

meta.title: Meta
meta.description: The metadata of the user resource.

schemas.title: Schemas
schemas.description: The schema of the user resource.

userName.title: UserName
userName.description: A unique identifier for the user to authenticate with the service provider directly. This attribute is required.

name.title: Name
name.description: The components of the user's name. This attribute may return:Full name as a single string Individual component attributes Both variants. The  formatted version indicates how the component attributes are combined so that both variants describe the same name.

displayName.title: DisplayName
displayName.description: The display name of the user, usually the full name.

emails.title: Emails
emails.description: The e-mail addresses of the user. The service provider uses canonical form of these addresses, for example, bjensen@example.com instead of bjensen@EXAMPLE.COM. Canonical type values are work, home, and other.

roles.title: Roles
roles.description: The list of roles for users in Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications.

active.title: Active
active.description: Indicates the administrative status of the user. The service provider determines the definitive value for this attribute. For example, true indicates that the user is able to login, and false indicates that the user account is suspended.

password.title: Password
password.description: The clear text password of the user. This attribute is used to specify an initial password for new users and to reset password for existing users. The password attribute is never returned in response to a query.

value.title: Value
value.description: The e-mail addresses of the user. The service provider uses canonical form of these addresses, for example, bjensen@example.com instead of bjensen@EXAMPLE.COM. Canonical type values are work, home, and other.

type.title: Type
type.description: The type of e-mail id, which is hard coded to work.

primary.title: Primary
primary.description: Indicates that this e-mail id is the primary address. This value is hard coded to true.

userCategory.title: UserCategory
userCategory.description: A user is associated with only one category, at any given time. The default user category value is DEFAULT.

accountType.title: AccountType
accountType.description: The account type of a user to indicate person or party association.

partyInformation.title: PartyInformation
partyInformation.description: The party information associated with the user.

workerInformation.title: WorkerInformation
workerInformation.description: The worker information associated with the user.

assignServiceAdminRoles.title: AssignServiceAdminRoles
assignServiceAdminRoles.description: Indicates if the service admin roles are assigned to the user. You can use this attribute to assign ORA_FND_DIAG_ADMINISTRATOR_JOB, ORA_ASM_APPLICATION_IMPLEMENTATION_CONSULTANT_JOB, and ORA_FND_IT_SECURITY_MANAGER_JOB admin roles when you create or update a user.

partyNumber.title: PartyNumber
partyNumber.description: The party number associated with the user.

PartyUsage.title: Party Usage
PartyUsage.description: The party usage associated with the user, for example, Supplier Contact,Partner Contact, and Customer Contact.

personNumber.title: PersonNumber
personNumber.description: The person number associated with the user.

manager.title: Manager
manager.description: The manager name of the user.

job.title: Job
job.description: The job title of the user.

businessUnit.title: BusinessUnit
businessUnit.description: The business unit to which the user belongs.

department.title: Department
department.description: The department to which the user belongs.

id.title: Id
id.description: A list of ids for the roles that a user can belong to, through direct membership.

value.title: Value
value.description: A list of values for the roles.

displayName.title: DisplayName
displayName.description: The display name of the role.
